        The Big Bang theory is the leading cosmological model describing the development of the universe from its earliest known periods through its subsequent large-scale evolution. At this point, all matter, energy, space, and time were compressed into an infinitely small point.

As it expanded, the energy began to condense into subatomic particles, which eventually formed the first atoms (primarily hydrogen and helium).
